risks: API Management Developer tier has no SLA and is not suitable for production workloads — only supports a single unit with no availability zone or multi-region support

Key Vault purge protection is disabled (enablePurgeProtection: false), contradicting CIS Azure Foundations Benchmark and Azure security best practices which recommend enabling it to prevent permanent data loss
No managed identity configuration for the App Service — best practices strongly recommend managed identities over key-based authentication for accessing Key Vault, Cosmos DB, OpenAI, and Storage
All resources have publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled with no VNet integration or private endpoints, increasing attack surface
No Application Insights or Log Analytics workspace included — centralized monitoring and observability infrastructure is missing
Cosmos DB has enableAutomaticFailover set to false and no geo-redundancy, creating a single point of failure
Storage account uses Standard_LRS with no geo-redundancy — documents cannot be recovered from a regional outage
correctionsRecommended: Add an Application Insights resource and Log Analytics workspace for centralized monitoring, and connect them to the App Service and APIM
Configure a system-assigned managed identity on the App Service and use it to authenticate to Key Vault, Cosmos DB, OpenAI, and Storage instead of key-based auth
Enable purge protection on the Key Vault (enablePurgeProtection: true) to comply with CIS benchmarks and prevent accidental or malicious permanent deletion
Upgrade API Management from Developer to Standard v2 or Premium v2 tier for production SLA, scaling, and network isolation capabilities
Add VNet integration for the App Service and configure private endpoints for Key Vault, Cosmos DB, OpenAI, and Storage to restrict public network access
Enable automatic failover on Cosmos DB (enableAutomaticFailover: true) for improved reliability
Consider upgrading Storage redundancy from Standard_LRS to Standard_GRS or Standard_ZRS for geo- or zone-redundant document protection
